entries.title: SQL Injection
entries.description: Improper neutralization of special elements used in an SQL command. Attackers can manipulate SQL queries to access, modify, or delete database contents.

entries.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
entries.description: Improper neutralization of input during web page generation. Attackers can inject client-side scripts into web pages viewed by other users.

entries.title: Use of Hardcoded Credentials
entries.description: The software contains hardcoded credentials such as passwords, API keys, or cryptographic keys. These can be extracted from source code, binaries, or configuration files.

entries.title: Use of Hardcoded Password
entries.description: The software contains a hardcoded password used for authentication, which may be recovered by an attacker from source code, binaries, or memory dumps.

entries.title: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
entries.description: The application stores sensitive information (credentials, tokens, personal data) in cleartext, making it accessible to anyone who can access the storage.

entries.title: Exposure of Sensitive Information
entries.description: The product exposes sensitive information to unauthorized actors. This includes API keys, tokens, internal URLs, debug information, or stack traces in error messages.

entries.title: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
entries.description: The product does not encrypt sensitive data before storage or transmission. Attackers can intercept network traffic or access stored data to obtain sensitive information.

entries.title: Use of Weak Cryptography
entries.description: The use of broken or weak cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, RC4, DES, 3DES) that can be practically attacked by adversaries.

entries.title: Path Traversal
entries.description: Improper limitation of a pathname to a restricted directory. Attackers can use '../' sequences to access files outside the intended directory.

entries.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
entries.description: The server fetches a URL controlled by the attacker. Attackers can make requests to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, or other restricted resources.

entries.title: OS Command Injection
entries.description: Improper neutralization of special elements used in an OS command. Attackers can execute arbitrary commands on the host operating system.

entries.title: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
entries.description: The application deserializes untrusted data without sufficient verification, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution or object injection.

entries.title: Improper Authentication
entries.description: When an actor claims a given identity, the software does not prove or insufficiently proves that the claim is correct. Attackers can bypass authentication.

entries.title: Missing Authorization
entries.description: The software does not perform an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action. Attackers can access resources without proper permissions.

entries.title: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
entries.description: The product transmits or stores authentication credentials in a way that allows interception or retrieval by unauthorized actors.

entries.title: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
entries.description: Sensitive information is transmitted in cleartext over the network. Attackers on the same network can intercept and read this data.

entries.title: Unrestricted Upload of Dangerous File
entries.description: The software allows uploading files without proper validation of file type, content, or size. Attackers can upload malicious scripts or executables.

entries.title: Improper Certificate Validation
entries.description: The software does not validate or incorrectly validates SSL/TLS certificates, enabling man-in-the-middle attacks.

entries.title: Open Redirect
entries.description: The web application accepts untrusted input to build a redirect URL, which can be exploited to redirect users to malicious sites.

entries.title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
entries.description: The web application does not verify that a request was intentionally sent by the user. Attackers can trick users into performing unwanted actions on authenticated sites.

entries.title: Weak Password Requirements
entries.description: The product does not require that users use strong passwords, making credential-based attacks more likely to succeed.

entries.title: Insufficient Session Expiration
entries.description: Session tokens remain valid for too long after the user logs out or becomes inactive, allowing session hijacking or reuse.

entries.title: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection
entries.description: The software processes XML documents containing external entity references, allowing attackers to read local files, perform SSRF, or cause DoS.

entries.title: Known Vulnerable npm Package
entries.description: A dependency in package.json has known security vulnerabilities. Regularly audit dependencies with `npm audit` and update to patched versions.

entries.title: Known Vulnerable Python Package
entries.description: A Python dependency has known security vulnerabilities. Use `pip-audit` or `safety` to scan for known vulnerable packages.

entries.title: Known Vulnerable Rust Crate
entries.description: A Rust crate dependency has known security vulnerabilities reported in the RustSec Advisory Database.

entries.title: Inefficient Regular Expression (ReDoS)
entries.description: The product uses a regular expression with worst-case exponential time complexity, which can be exploited for denial-of-service attacks.

entries.title: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption (DoS)
entries.description: The software does not properly control the allocation and maintenance of limited resources, enabling denial-of-service attacks.

entries.title: Allocation of Resources Without Limits
entries.description: The software allocates resources without enforcing limits, which can lead to resource exhaustion and denial of service.

entries.title: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
entries.description: The software generates error messages that include sensitive information about its environment, users, or associated data.

entries.title: Hardcoded Secret Best Practices
entries.description: Hardcoded secrets in source code are a common security anti-pattern. Secrets committed to version control remain accessible forever in git history.

entries.title: Dependency Management Best Practices
entries.description: Outdated or vulnerable dependencies are a leading cause of security breaches in modern applications. Supply chain attacks exploit known vulnerabilities in dependencies.

entries.title: Code Injection
entries.description: Improper control of generation of code (code injection). Attackers can inject and execute arbitrary code in the application context.

entries.title: Improper Input Validation
entries.description: The product does not validate or incorrectly validates input that can affect the control flow or data flow of the program.

entries.title: Command Injection
entries.description: Improper neutralization of special elements used in a command. Attackers can execute arbitrary commands on the host system.

entries.title: LDAP Injection
entries.description: Improper neutralization of special elements used in LDAP queries. Attackers can modify LDAP queries to bypass authentication or access unauthorized data.

entries.title: XPath Injection
entries.description: Improper neutralization of data within XPath expressions. Attackers can manipulate XPath queries to access unauthorized XML data.

entries.title: External Control of File Name or Path
entries.description: The software allows user input to control or influence file paths used in filesystem operations, enabling path traversal or arbitrary file access.

entries.title: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
entries.description: The software does not perform authentication for functionality that requires a provable user identity or consumes significant resources.

entries.title: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
entries.description: The software does not limit the number of failed authentication attempts, enabling brute-force attacks against credentials.

entries.title: Session Fixation
entries.description: Authenticating a user without invalidating any existing session identifier gives an attacker the opportunity to steal authenticated sessions.

entries.title: Unverified Password Change
entries.description: The password change functionality does not require the current password or other verification, allowing attackers to change passwords without authorization.

entries.title: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
entries.description: The system's authorization does not prevent a user from accessing another user's data by modifying a key (IDOR).

entries.title: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
entries.description: The product specifies permissions for security-critical resources in a way that allows unintended actors to read or modify them.

entries.title: Inadequate Encryption Strength
entries.description: The software stores or transmits sensitive data using an encryption scheme that is theoretically sound but not strong enough for the level of protection required.

entries.title: Use of Weak Hash
entries.description: The software uses a cryptographic hash function that is known to be weak (MD5, SHA-1). Attackers can create hash collisions or perform faster brute-force attacks.

entries.title: Use of Cryptographically Weak PRNG
entries.description: Using a non-cryptographically secure pseudo-random number generator in a security context can produce predictable values that attackers can guess.

entries.title: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
entries.description: The software does not verify or incorrectly verifies the cryptographic signature for data, allowing tampering or forgery.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Open Redirect in Express
entries.description: Express.js applications using res.redirect() with user-supplied URLs without validation are vulnerable to open redirect attacks.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Prototype Pollution in Node.js
entries.description: Improperly handled recursive merge or object assignment functions in Node.js can allow attackers to inject properties into Object.prototype.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Formula Injection in CSV Export
entries.description: CSV exports that include user-controlled data starting with =, +, -, or @ can trigger formula injection when opened in Excel or Google Sheets.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Unsafe Rust Block
entries.description: Unsafe Rust blocks bypass the compiler's safety guarantees and can lead to memory corruption, undefined behavior, and security vulnerabilities.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Unwrapped Results in Rust
entries.description: Using .unwrap() or .expect() on Result/Option types in production code can cause panics that crash the application, leading to denial of service.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Unsafe Deserialization with pickle
entries.description: Python's pickle module can execute arbitrary code during deserialization. Deserializing untrusted pickle data is extremely dangerous.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Unsafe YAML Loading
entries.description: Using yaml.load() (without SafeLoader) can execute arbitrary Python code embedded in YAML files. This is the default in older PyYAML versions.

entries.title: Language-Specific: NoSQL Injection in MongoDB
entries.description: Passing user-controlled objects directly to MongoDB queries (e.g., { $gt: '' } as a password) can bypass authentication or filter conditions.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Catastrophic Backtracking in Node.js
entries.description: Node.js runs on a single-threaded event loop. A regex with nested quantifiers matching malicious input can block the entire server.

entries.title: Language-Specific: SQL Injection via fmt.Sprintf in Go
entries.description: Using fmt.Sprintf() to build SQL queries with user input bypasses Go's database/sql parameterization, enabling SQL injection.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Java Deserialization Attacks
entries.description: Java's ObjectInputStream deserializes arbitrary objects from untrusted data. Gadget chains in common libraries (Commons Collections, Spring) can lead to RCE.

entries.title: Language-Specific: Local File Inclusion in PHP
entries.description: Using include() or require() with user-controlled paths allows attackers to include arbitrary files, leading to code execution or information disclosure.

entries.title: OWASP Mobile M1: Improper Credential Usage
entries.description: Mobile apps with hardcoded credentials, API keys, or tokens can be reverse-engineered to extract these secrets.

entries.title: OWASP Mobile M2: Insecure Data Storage
entries.description: Storing sensitive data in plaintext in SharedPreferences, NSUserDefaults, SQLite databases, or external storage exposes data to other apps or device theft.

entries.title: OWASP Mobile M3: Insecure Communication
entries.description: Mobile apps that communicate over HTTP or accept any TLS certificate are vulnerable to MITM attacks that can steal credentials and data.

entries.title: CI/CD Pipeline: Secret in Build Logs
entries.description: Secrets echoed or logged during CI/CD builds remain accessible in build logs, which are often readable by anyone with repository access.

entries.title: Cloud IAM: Overly Permissive IAM Policies
entries.description: IAM policies with wildcard actions (*) or resources (*) grant excessive permissions. A compromised service account with admin access can destroy infrastructure.

entries.title: Cloud Storage: Public S3 Bucket
entries.description: S3 buckets or cloud storage containers configured with public read/write access expose sensitive data to the internet.

entries.title: Container Security: Secrets in Dockerfile
entries.description: Hardcoding secrets in Dockerfiles (ENV API_KEY=xxx) embeds them in image layers, accessible to anyone with image access or via docker history.

entries.title: Kubernetes Security: Privileged Container
entries.description: Containers running with privileged: true or securityContext.privileged can escape container isolation and access the host system.

entries.title: API Security: Missing Rate Limiting
entries.description: APIs without rate limiting can be abused for brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, data scraping, or denial of service.

entries.title: API Security: Missing Request Size Limits
entries.description: APIs that accept arbitrarily large request bodies can be exploited for memory exhaustion, disk filling, or DoS attacks.

entries.title: Secret Rotation Best Practices
entries.description: Secrets that are never rotated remain valid indefinitely. If leaked, they provide permanent access until manually revoked.

entries.title: Secure Logging Best Practices
entries.description: Logging sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, credit card numbers) creates a secondary attack surface where attackers can find secrets in log files.

entries.title: Secure Error Handling Best Practices
entries.description: Detailed error messages, stack traces, and debug output expose internal implementation details that attackers can use to identify vulnerabilities.

entries.title: Content Security Policy Best Practices
entries.description: Missing or weak Content-Security-Policy headers leave applications vulnerable to XSS, data injection, and clickjacking attacks.

entries.title: CORS Configuration Best Practices
entries.description: Misconfigured CORS headers can allow malicious websites to make authenticated cross-origin requests, stealing data or performing actions on behalf of users.

entries.title: Supply Chain Security Best Practices
entries.description: Modern applications depend on hundreds or thousands of third-party packages. A single compromised dependency can give attackers access to the entire application.
